title: I Rest Because I Am Valuable
subtitle: An energy statement for releasing the belief that rest must be earned
excerpt: A three-minute energy statement from Day 26 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The statement releases guilt and the idea that stopping requires justification, and allows permission to rest as something inherent — not a reward for enough done.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 26 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795). The release-and-allow structure draws from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).

practice.whenToUse: Use when guilt about resting is present — mid-afternoon when you want to stop but can't justify it, or in the evening when the not-enough-done feeling is loud.

content.text: A three-minute energy statement from Day 26 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The statement targets the belief that rest is a reward for productivity: releasing the guilt and the sense that stopping requires justification, and allowing permission to rest as something you already have.
Say the release statement three times, then the allow statement three times. Read it aloud if possible — the spoken word carries more weight than reading silently.
I release all guilt about resting. I release every belief that rest must be earned. I release the idea that stopping makes me irresponsible.
I allow myself to rest freely. I allow permission that comes from what I am, not what I produce. I rest because I am valuable — and that was always enough.
Adapted from the Day 26 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The release-and-allow structure draws from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).
